vocabulary.meaning: picture, short film, or text that tries to sell something
vocabulary.example: I saw an advertisement for a new smartphone on TV.

vocabulary.meaning: have enough money or time to be able to buy or do something
vocabulary.example: We wonder if he could afford a new car.

vocabulary.meaning: something on sale at a lower price than its true value
vocabulary.example: At only $5, this shirt was a real bargain.

vocabulary.meaning: type of product made by a particular company under a particular name
vocabulary.example: What brand of washing powder do you normally buy?

vocabulary.meaning: book or magazine showing a list of items you can buy
vocabulary.example: She looked through the clothing catalogue to find a new coat.

vocabulary.meaning: coins rather than paper money, or money returned when you pay with a larger bill
vocabulary.example: Here is your ticket and your change, sir.

vocabulary.meaning: small, round piece of metal used as money
vocabulary.example: He flipped a coin to decide who would go first.

vocabulary.meaning: amount of money needed to buy or do something; require payment of an amount
vocabulary.example: How much does this laptop cost? / The cost of living has risen.

vocabulary.meaning: person who buys goods or services from a shop or business
vocabulary.example: The shop assistant was helping a customer find a dress.

vocabulary.meaning: amount of money that you owe to someone
vocabulary.example: He spent years trying to pay off his credit card debt.

vocabulary.meaning: ask for something forcefully, in a way that shows you expect it to be done
vocabulary.example: The workers demanded a pay rise from their manager.

vocabulary.meaning: send goods or services to another country for sale
vocabulary.example: Vietnam exports a large amount of rice every year.

vocabulary.meaning: money paid to a professional person or organization for their work
vocabulary.example: The school fees must be paid at the beginning of the term.

vocabulary.meaning: large amount of money or wealth
vocabulary.example: He made a fortune by investing in the stock market.

vocabulary.meaning: bring goods or services into a country from abroad for sale
vocabulary.example: The country imports most of its oil from overseas.

vocabulary.meaning: put money into a project or business to make a profit
vocabulary.example: He decided to invest his savings in real estate.

vocabulary.meaning: get or acquire something, especially by making an effort
vocabulary.example: You must obtain permission from the council before building.

vocabulary.meaning: need to pay or give something to someone because they have lent it to you
vocabulary.example: I owe my brother ten dollars for the ticket.

vocabulary.meaning: have something that legally belongs to you
vocabulary.example: They own a beautiful house by the lake.

vocabulary.meaning: unit of liquid measure equal to about 473 ml (in US) or 568 ml (in UK)
vocabulary.example: Please buy a pint of milk on your way home.

vocabulary.meaning: money that you earn in business after paying all costs
vocabulary.example: The company made a huge profit this quarter.

vocabulary.meaning: land and buildings owned by someone, or objects belonging to someone
vocabulary.example: This building is private property; trespassing is forbidden.

vocabulary.meaning: buy something; act of buying something
vocabulary.example: She purchased a new laptop yesterday.

vocabulary.meaning: document showing that money has been paid for goods
vocabulary.example: Keep your receipt in case you want to return the dress.

vocabulary.meaning: need something or make something necessary
vocabulary.example: The job requires excellent communication skills.

vocabulary.meaning: period during which a shop sells goods at reduced prices
vocabulary.example: I bought these shoes half-price in the winter sale.

vocabulary.meaning: keep money for future use rather than spending it
vocabulary.example: She is trying to save money to buy a new phone.

vocabulary.meaning: choose someone or something carefully from a group
vocabulary.example: Please select the option that best describes you.

vocabulary.meaning: provide something that is needed; amount of something available for use
vocabulary.example: The company supplies office equipment to local businesses.

vocabulary.meaning: many different types of things or people
vocabulary.example: The market offers a wide variety of fresh fruits.

vocabulary.meaning: use more money, time, or energy than is useful; bad use of something
vocabulary.example: Don't waste your money on things you don't need.

vocabulary.meaning: think about problems or unpleasant things that might happen in a way that makes you anxious
vocabulary.example: Don't worry about the exam; you have studied hard.

vocabulary.meaning: small amount of money that parents give to children regularly
vocabulary.example: He gets five dollars of pocket money every week.

vocabulary.meaning: person whose job is to guide people about the law and speak for them in court
vocabulary.example: She hired a lawyer to help her with the contract.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: calculate the total of two or more numbers
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: If you add up all these numbers, you get 100.
EX: We just came back from our holiday in Paris yesterday.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: give something to someone without asking for money
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: They are giving away free samples of the new shampoo.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: do something more quickly because there is not much time
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Hurry up, or we will miss the train!

phrasalVerbs.meaning: return money that you have borrowed from someone
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: I will pay you back next week when I get my salary.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: keep money regularly to buy something specific in the future
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: She is saving up for a trip to Japan next summer.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: return something you have bought to a shop, or return an object to its owner
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: If the dress doesn't fit, you can take it back to the shop.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: remove something that is on a wall or high place
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: They took down the party decorations the day after the event.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: method of paying using a card or check
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: Would you like to pay in cash or by credit card?

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: available to be rented, like a house or room
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: There is a small flat for rent near the university.
EX: That old house on the corner is finally for sale.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: paying with paper notes and coins rather than a card
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: He paid for the groceries in cash.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: owing money to someone or a bank
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: He ended up deep in debt after his business failed.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: state of repair or health of an object or person
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: The second-hand car is still in very good condition.

wordFormation.meaning: add: put together to increase size or number / addition: act of adding or a mathematical calculation
wordFormation.example:
EX: You can add some sugar to the tea.
EX: In addition to his salary, he gets a monthly bonus.

wordFormation.meaning: affordable: cheap enough for most people to buy
wordFormation.example: EX: The company aims to make housing more affordable.

wordFormation.meaning: compare: estimate, measure, or note the similarity or dissimilarity between / comparison: act of comparing two or more things
wordFormation.example:
EX: You can compare the prices of these two laptops.
EX: The document makes a direct comparison between the two systems.

wordFormation.meaning: decide: make a choice after thinking carefully / decisive: able to make decisions quickly and confidently / decision: choice or judgment made after thinking
wordFormation.example:
EX: I can't decide which shirt to buy.
EX: She is a decisive leader who gets things done.
EX: He made a quick decision to join the club.

wordFormation.meaning: expense: cost required for something / (in)expensive: costing (little) a lot of money
wordFormation.example:
EX: Traveling abroad can be a huge expense.
EX: This restaurant is delicious but very expensive.

wordFormation.meaning: judge: form an opinion about someone or something / judgement: opinion or decision made after thinking carefully
wordFormation.example:
EX: Don't judge a book by its cover.
EX: In my judgement, this is the best course of action.

wordFormation.meaning: service: act of helping or doing work for someone / servant: person who works in another person's house
wordFormation.example:
EX: The hotel provides excellent customer service.
EX: The wealthy family hired two servants to clean the house.

wordFormation.meaning: truth: quality of being true; facts / untrue: not true; false / truthful: honest and always telling the truth
wordFormation.example:
EX: Tell me the truth about what happened.
EX: The rumors about his resignation were untrue.
EX: She gave a truthful account of the accident.

wordFormation.meaning: useful: helping you to do or achieve something / useless: not helping you to do or achieve anything
wordFormation.example:
EX: The guidebook was very useful during our trip.
EX: A broken umbrella is completely useless in the rain.

wordFormation.meaning: valuable: worth a lot of money or very useful
wordFormation.example: EX: The museum contains many valuable paintings.

wordPatterns.example: EX: I was completely wrong about his intentions.

wordPatterns.meaning: there is a problem or issue with something
wordPatterns.example: EX: There is something wrong with my computer; it won't turn on.
EX: This laptop belongs to the school.

wordPatterns.meaning: take something from someone with the promise to return it
wordPatterns.example: EX: Can I borrow a pen from you?

wordPatterns.meaning: get something by paying money to someone
wordPatterns.example: EX: I bought this jacket from a local vintage shop.

wordPatterns.meaning: make a choice between two or more options
wordPatterns.example: EX: She had to choose between studying law or medicine.

wordPatterns.meaning: examine two things to note their similarities or differences
wordPatterns.example: EX: You can't compare my work with hers; they are completely different.

wordPatterns.meaning: make a final choice about something after thinking
wordPatterns.example: EX: We finally decided on the blue paint for the kitchen.

wordPatterns.meaning: give something to someone for a short time, expecting it back
wordPatterns.example: EX: He lent his bicycle to his cousin for the weekend.

wordPatterns.meaning: give money to get goods or services
wordPatterns.example: EX: Who is going to pay for these drinks?

wordPatterns.meaning: use money or time on a specific activity or item
wordPatterns.example: EX: She spends a lot of money on books.

wordPatterns.meaning: notice or picture promoting a product or service
wordPatterns.example: EX: I saw a funny advertisement for a new soda brand.
